L1.area: Internal utility and helper library functions

L2.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where UUID generation functions silently fall back to producing predictable, all-zero identifiers when the underlying secure random number generator fails to provide entropy. No error is surfaced to callers, allowing security-critical code paths such as session management, CSRF protection, and rate limiting to unknowingly rely on colliding, predictable identifiers.

L3.area: Internal UUID generation utilities within the framework's vendored helper library
L3.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where UUID generation functions silently fall back to producing predictable, all-zero identifiers when the underlying secure random number generator fails to provide entropy on Go versions prior to 1.24. Instead of signaling the failure, the functions return a constant zero-value UUID, allowing security-critical middleware components such as session management, CSRF tokens, and request ID generation to unknowingly rely on colliding, predictable identifiers.

ground_truth.reason: UUID generation functions silently fall back to returning a predictable all-zero UUID when crypto/rand fails to provide entropy, instead of propagating the error to callers.
